meta.mode: GOVERNANCE / DESIGN ONLY — no runtime code, no executor implemented
principles: Shell-first: prefer mechanical shell work; AI for logic/sequencing only.
Human approval required before ANY execution — non-autonomous by construction.
Verification-first: every batch ends in a verification gate.
Rollback-safe: every executed step must be revertible (checkpoint or inverse command).
No secrets printed or interpolated into commands or logs.
One phase at a time; no multi-blocker fan-out.
Replit-safe execution only; respects platform constraints.
Domain separation preserved: no auth/healing/crisis/journal/provider/billing/admin/chat runtime changes; no business logic in healing flows.

commandQueueModel.description: An ordered, append-only queue of proposed shell commands. The manager NEVER auto-executes; it proposes, a human approves, then a single approved step runs, is verified, and the result gates the next step.
lifecycle: APPROVE/REJECT -> human sets approvalState (rejected items never run)
EXECUTE -> single approved, dependency-satisfied item runs (executionState=running)
VERIFY -> verification gate runs; failure halts the queue
GATE -> on success, the next item becomes eligible; on failure, queue pauses for human decision
invariants: At most ONE item executing at a time (no parallel mutation).
An item runs only if approvalState=approved AND all dependsOn succeeded.
destructive-blocked items can never be approved by the manager; they are routed to a human-only escalation path.
A failed verification gate freezes the queue; no further auto-progress.

mutatingRequiresApproval: writing/editing a single file via the file tools
allowedCommands.notes: Allowed != automatic. Every mutating command still requires explicit human approval. Read-only commands may run for diagnosis but are still logged.

blockedDangerousCommands.policy: These are NEVER executed by the manager under any approval state. They are recognized, refused, and routed to a human-only manual path with explicit out-of-band confirmation.
patterns: mv / rename of existing tracked files (treated as destructive move)

git commit, git add, git reset, git restore, git checkout, git clean, git rebase, git revert, git rm, git push --force, git filter-branch, git reflog expire, git gc --prune, git update-ref -d, git init
kill -9 of platform-managed workflow PIDs / pkill broad patterns
curl | sh, wget | bash (remote code execution)
:(){ :|:& };: (fork bomb) and any shell that pipes downloaded content to an interpreter
writing to or echoing secret/env values; printenv of secret keys
blockedDangerousCommands.escalation: A blocked command produces a refusal record + a human-only proposal note. The platform's auto-checkpoint and (where required) a background Project Task are the only sanctioned routes for destructive git operations.

approvalGates.model: Two-key, human-in-the-loop. The agent proposes; a human approves; only then does a single step run.

gates.rule: Every command classified read-only/mutating/destructive-blocked BEFORE proposal. Misclassification = fail-closed (treat as destructive-blocked).
Mutating commands require explicit human approval (approvalState=approved). No implicit/time-based approval.
Item runs only when all dependsOn items succeeded.
Only one mutating command executes at a time.
destructive-blocked commands cannot be approved here; human-only out-of-band path required.

approvalGates.expiry: Approvals are scoped to a single queue item and expire if the queue context changes (new dependency, edited command).

verify:all.pass: Summary: NNN pass, 0 fail (warns allowed, e.g. pre-existing copyright WARN)

verificationGates.rule: A batch is GREEN only if all applicable gates pass. Any failure freezes the queue (no auto-progress) and surfaces the failing gate to the human.

strategies.name: checkpoint
strategies.how: Rely on Replit auto-checkpoint; record the commit id; revert by suggesting rollback to that checkpoint.

strategies.name: inverse-command
strategies.when: additive shell mutation with a clean inverse
strategies.how: Store inverseCommand (e.g. created dir -> rmdir of that exact dir). Inverse must itself be non-destructive and approved.

invariants: No step executes unless a rollback strategy is recorded.
Rollback never uses blocked destructive commands; checkpoint rollback is preferred.
A failed step that left partial state must be rolled back before the queue resumes.

loggingFormat.sink: codex/automation/logs/scm-<date>.jsonl (append-only; created in a FUTURE implementation phase, not now)
loggingFormat.redaction: Secrets/env values are NEVER logged. stdout/stderr are stored as digests with secret-like tokens masked.
loggingFormat.outputContract: Per kernel: What changed (files+lines) / Before / After / Build status / Next step. No speculation.

constraints: git commit/add and all destructive git are blocked for the main agent; the platform auto-checkpoints after each loop. Treat checkpoints as the commit mechanism.

Background servers launched from the shell are reaped when the call returns (exit 143). Use the platform-managed 'Start application' workflow for a persistent server; run route checks against 127.0.0.1:5000.
Port 5000 is owned by the dev workflow and auto-reclaimed; do not fight it with kill/pkill.
Never edit package.json, vite.config.*, server/vite.ts, server/app.mjs, .replit, drizzle.config.ts.
DB schema changes go through Drizzle + npm run db:push (never hand-written SQL); --force only with explicit approval (data-loss).
Secrets are managed via the platform; never echo or interpolate them.
replitSafeWorkflow.executionLoop: propose (agent) -> approve (human) -> run ONE step -> verify (build/verify:all/route-check) -> gate -> next. Stop on any red.

inputs: codex/storage/*.json (audit, hotspots, candidates, blockers, remediation plan) as command-source-of-truth for storage-guard passes
scripts/storage/auditStorageUsage.mjs as the canonical regeneration command
outputs: regenerated codex/storage/*.{json,md} when a guard pass mutates runtime code
codexIntegrationPoints.alignment: SCM sequences exactly the kind of one-file, verify-after-each-step storage-guard passes already performed in H2.8–H2.16, formalizing them into an approved, logged, rollback-safe queue.

futureImplementationPlan.note: Implementation is NOT part of this phase. Sequenced for future, separately-approved phases.

phases.title: Schema + validators
phases.scope: Encode queueItem schema + classifier (read-only/mutating/destructive-blocked) as a pure, tested module. No execution.

phases.title: Dry-run proposer
phases.scope: Generate a proposed queue from a codex plan (e.g. remediation plan) WITHOUT executing. Output a human-readable approval sheet.

phases.title: Approval ledger
phases.scope: Append-only approval/refusal log + redacted logging sink. Still no execution.

phases.title: Single-flight executor (guarded)
phases.scope: Execute ONE approved, non-destructive step, then run verification gate. Hard-refuse blocked patterns. Checkpoint-based rollback.

phases.title: Route + build gate integration
phases.scope: Wire build/verify:all/route-check as automatic post-step gates that freeze the queue on red.

futureImplementationPlan.exitCriteria: Each future phase ends GREEN (verify:all 0 fail) and is independently rollback-safe.
